title: Add multiple search backends with API-key rotation and Anthropic-native search (parity with CC WebSearchTool)
description:
Gap: CC WebSearchTool delegates to Anthropic's native web_search_20250305 server tool (WebSearchTool.ts:76-84, :254-399). The API itself performs the searching. Multiple searches per call are supported (max 8, WebSearchTool.ts:83). Results are typed SearchResult[] with title+url per hit plus interleaved text commentary. Enabled only on firstParty, vertex (claude-4+ models), and foundry providers (WebSearchTool.ts:168-193).

OC counterpart: OC calls third-party APIs in priority order — Tavily → Brave Search → DuckDuckGo headless browser (src/web.rs:338-364). No API-key rotation between backends. No provider-gating. DuckDuckGo path requires the 'browser' cargo feature. Single backend executes per call; no multi-search streaming.

Additional divergences: CC emits streaming query_update and search_results_received progress events during the search (WebSearchTool.ts:299-387); OC is synchronous with no progress reporting. CC appends a mandatory citation reminder to every result block (WebSearchTool.ts:427); OC format_search_results does not.

Done when: OC supports the Anthropic native search tool when the active provider is firstParty/vertex/foundry; existing Tavily/Brave/DDG path retained as fallback for other providers.

Parent spec: #529
